intended_use: DV.Target is a software application that enables the routing of DICOM-compliant data (CT Images) to automatic image processing workflows, using machine learning-based algorithms to automatically delin
intended_use_full: DV.Target is a software application that enables the routing of DICOM-compliant data (CT Images) to automatic image processing workflows, using machine learning-based algorithms to automatically delineate organs-at-risk (OARs). Contours generated by DV.Target may be used as an input to clinical workflows for treatment planning in radiation therapy. DV.Target is intended to be used by trained medical professionals including radiologists, radiation oncologists, dosimetrists, and physicists. DV.Target does not provide a user interface for data visualization. Image data uploaded, auto-contouring results, and other functionalities are managed via an administration interface. Thus, it is required that DV.Target be used in conjunction with appropriate software, such as a treatment planning system (TPS), to review, edit, and approve for all contours generated by DV.Target. DV.Target is only intended for normal organ contouring, not for tumor or clinical target volume contouring.
target_condition: Organs-at-risk delineation for radiation therapy treatment planning
ground_truth: Ground truth OARs contours on public validation data were generated from consensus of three board-certified physicians; ground truth contours on in-house clinical data were based on actual clinical contouring results
other_metrics: Dice-Sørensen coefficient (DICE score) reported for contouring accuracy; DV.Target demonstrated non-inferiority to predicate device (Mirada Workflow Box) on 19 overlapping OARs and non-inferiority to reference device (MIM – MRT Dosimetry) on 30 non-overlapping OARs; confidence intervals of performance differences were within non-inferiority margin for all compared OARs
